controls.title: Organisational Context
controls.summary: The circumstances — mission, stakeholder expectations, dependencies, legal/regulatory requirements — surrounding the organisation's cybersecurity risk management decisions are understood.

controls.title: Risk Management Strategy
controls.summary: Organisational priorities, constraints, risk tolerance and appetite statements, and assumptions are established, communicated, and used to support operational risk decisions.

controls.title: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities
controls.summary: Cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and authorities to foster accountability, performance assessment, and continuous improvement are established and communicated.

controls.title: Policy
controls.summary: Organisational cybersecurity policy is established, communicated, and enforced based on risk strategy and regulatory requirements.

controls.title: Oversight
controls.summary: Results of organisation-wide cybersecurity risk management activities and performance are used to inform, improve, and adjust the risk management strategy.

controls.title: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.summary: Cybersecurity risks associated with the supply chain are identified, established, managed, monitored, and improved throughout the supplier and third-party partner lifecycle.

controls.title: Asset Management
controls.summary: Assets (data, hardware, software, services, physical, personnel) that enable the organisation to achieve business purposes are identified and managed consistent with their importance.

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.summary: The cybersecurity risk to the organisation, assets, and individuals is understood by the organisation. Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and impacts are identified and prioritised.

controls.title: Improvement
controls.summary: Improvements to cybersecurity risk management are identified from evaluations, assessments, and lessons learned, and implemented via action plans.

controls.title: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.summary: Access to physical and logical assets is limited to authorised users, services, and hardware and is managed commensurate with risk. Includes MFA, least privilege, and privileged access management.

controls.title: Awareness and Training
controls.summary: The organisation's personnel are provided cybersecurity awareness and training so they can perform their cybersecurity-related tasks effectively.

controls.title: Data Security
controls.summary: Data are managed consistent with risk strategy to protect confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information. Includes encryption at rest and in transit, DRM, and data classification.

controls.title: Platform Security
controls.summary: The hardware, software, and services of physical and virtual platforms are managed consistent with risk strategy to protect confidentiality, integrity, and availability. Includes hardening, patch management, and SBOM.

controls.title: Technology Infrastructure Resilience
controls.summary: Security architectures are managed consistent with risk to protect assets, including network segmentation, redundancy, failover, and zero-trust architecture principles.

controls.title: Continuous Monitoring
controls.summary: Assets are monitored to find anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events. Includes SIEM, EDR, NDR, log aggregation, and threat intelligence integration.

controls.title: Adverse Event Analysis
controls.summary: Anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events are analysed to characterise the events and detect cybersecurity incidents.

controls.title: Incident Management
controls.summary: Responses to detected cybersecurity incidents are managed. Includes incident response plan, playbooks, communication, and escalation procedures.

controls.title: Incident Analysis
controls.summary: Investigations are conducted to ensure effective response and support forensics and recovery activities. Root cause analysis is performed.

controls.title: Incident Response Reporting and Communication
controls.summary: Response activities are communicated to relevant internal and external stakeholders, including regulators, customers, and media, in accordance with regulatory obligations.

controls.title: Incident Recovery Plan Execution
controls.summary: Restoration activities are performed to ensure operational availability of systems and services affected by cybersecurity incidents, in accordance with the recovery plan.

controls.title: Incident Recovery Communication
controls.summary: Restoration activities are communicated to relevant parties including executive team, IT operations, owners, and affected customers, coordinating recovery steps.
